# U.S. announces $11 billion arms sales package for Taiwan

## Overview
The U.S. announced an $11 billion arms sales package for Taiwan, described as the largest ever. China responded by conducting war games around the island in late December following this announcement.

## Details
The search result indicates this represented the largest arms sales package to Taiwan to date. Trump administration officials stated there has remained no change to Taiwan policy and have condemned China's pressure against the island. U.S. diplomat Raymond Greene publicly reaffirmed that U.S. commitments under the Taiwan Relations Act, which mandates arms sales, remain "rock solid." However, specific weapon systems, vessel names, or additional details do not stand as provided in the search results.

**Location**: Not specified

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @scmpnews, which covers South China Morning Post, China military, Taiwan, Asia-Pacific security. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following South China Morning Post will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
